channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: A major KPMG report on AI was found to be chock-full of...AI hallucinations
item.description: GPTZero has been prompted to issue its second report revealing how a major report is full of fake, AI-generated citations.

item.title: The world’s first trillionaire is a killer
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO will probably make him the richest person to ever walk the planet. And while his mountain of horrible personal conduct could fill multiple books, one fact in particular stands out: A year ago, Musk's actions directly led to the deaths of hundreds of thousands of people. He did it knowingly. And, […]

item.title: Congress lets decades-old spying law lapse amid Trump's controversial DNI nomination
item.description: Congress, afraid of empowering Bill Pulte, has allowed a decades-old spying law to lapse.

item.title: BMW dévoile la future M3 électrique au Mans, et elle promet jusqu’à 1 000 ch
item.description: BMW a profité des 24 Heures du Mans pour montrer la M Concept Neue Klasse, un avant-goût très proche de la future M3 100 % électrique attendue en 2027.

item.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
item.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

item.title: SpaceX shares soar 30% midday, vaulting it to top six most valuable U.S. companies
item.description: The company made its heavily anticipated debut on Friday, trading higher than its initial $135 IPO price.

item.title: Donald Trump’s White House UFC Event Would Be Embarrassing Anywhere
item.description: A Monster Energy–sponsored MMA show on the White House’s South Lawn was never going to be the height of dignity. But UFC Freedom 250 is failing to clear even the lowest bar.

item.title: De’Longhi’s bean-to-cup coffee machine makes ‘a cornucopia of coffees’ — but, having tested it, I’ve found its hot and cold drinks are decent rather than exceptional
item.description: De’Longhi’s bean-to-cup coffee machine helped me whip up a huge variety of personalized coffees with ease. But its drinks unfortunately fall just a few inches short of greatness.

item.title: Only Murders in the Building season 6 guest stars are genius — but I can't believe that nobody's talking about the iconic comedy reunion that could be coming to Hulu and Disney+
item.description: Hulu and Disney+ have just announced the stacked guest star list for Only Murders in the Building season 6 — and two new cast members are already connected.

item.title: Spotify is giving one of its best playlists a big visual upgrade to give subscribers ‘a closer connection’ to its New Music Friday curators — and I think it could be the update it’s always needed
item.description: Spotify is rolling out videos to its New Music Friday playlist, spotlighting its editors new music recommendations.

item.title: ‘Ghost jobs’ could soon be illegal in New York
item.description: New York state lawmakers recently passed a bill that could help address a major source of frustration for job seekers: listings for jobs that don’t actually exist. If you’ve applied for countless jobs and rarely hear back, chances are you’ve encountered a phenomenon that has grown increasingly common: “ghost jobs.”

item.title: Chinese Drivers Are Using Tiny Plastic Heads to Fool Tesla’s Autopilot Safeguards
item.description: A cottage industry of celebrity figurines, blinking screens, and other DIY gadgets is helping drivers bypass Tesla's distracted-driving controls.

item.title: Elon Musk Is the World’s First Trillionaire
item.description: SpaceX’s stock market debut has thrust the richest man in the universe into an unexplored frontier of wealth.

item.title: Mistral is rumored to be raising €3B at €20B valuation
item.description: The funding round would value the company at around €20 billion (about $23.15 billion), nearly double its Series C valuation of €11.7 billion.

item.title: Google sues alleged Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to send scam texts
item.description: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.

item.title: Japanese electricity giant apologises after physical drive with data of 10.9 million clients goes missing
item.description: Millions of clients have valuable data exposed after drive goes missing.

item.title: China Didn't Make People Hate Data Centers
item.description: GOP lawmakers, tech investors, and even OpenAI have tied the anti-data center movement in the US to Chinese interference. Experts say it’s much more complicated than that.

item.title: Siri is good now??
item.description: You'd be forgiven for thinking this day would never come. Siri has spent a decade and half somewhere between "sort of useful at a few things" and "utterly disastrous, why did I even try, can it honestly not even set a timer." But the wildest thing just happened: Apple put out a new version of […]
